title: Vivid Crispy Mediterranean Pappardelle Pasta
description: A brunch‑friendly Mediterranean pasta that balances umami and crispiness while staying light on calories.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil, then cook the pappardelle according to package directions until al dente; drain and set aside.
While the pasta cooks, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat and add the minced garlic and sliced onion, sautéing until fragrant and translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the cherry tomatoes to the skillet, cooking until they begin to soften and release juices, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the sliced olives, capers, dried oregano, smoked paprika, and lemon juice, allowing the mixture to simmer for 2 minutes to meld flavors.
Toss the drained pasta into the skillet, coating it evenly with the tomato‑olive sauce, and cook for an additional 2 minutes so the pasta absorbs the umami.
Remove from heat and stir in the chopped parsley and basil, then sprinkle grated Parmesan over the top.
Transfer the pasta to a serving dish, ensuring it remains slightly crispy from the skillet, and serve immediately with a wedge of lemon if desired.
For an extra crunch, lightly toast 1 tsp of extra virgin olive oil in a separate pan, then drizzle over the finished pasta before plating.
